sub_pixel_system.notes: No sub-pixel system documentation available for PC version

special_mechanics.name: Climber
special_mechanics.description: Lemmings with Climber skill climb vertically but fall down if they hit an overhang

special_mechanics.name: Floater
special_mechanics.description: Uses a parachute to fall safely from heights, reducing fall damage

special_mechanics.name: Bomber
special_mechanics.description: Explodes after a five-second timer, destroying itself and any destructible landscape in close proximity

special_mechanics.name: Blocker
special_mechanics.description: Stands still and prevents other lemmings from passing; lemmings that hit a Blocker reverse direction

special_mechanics.name: Builder
special_mechanics.description: Builds a stairway of 12 steps; stops building when it hits its head, runs out of bricks, or stairway hits solid object

special_mechanics.name: Basher
special_mechanics.description: Tunnels horizontally; cannot break through steel barriers

special_mechanics.name: Miner
special_mechanics.description: Tunnels diagonally downwards; cannot break through steel barriers

special_mechanics.name: Digger
special_mechanics.description: Tunnels directly downwards; cannot break through steel barriers

tier_notes: Tier 2 - Empirical/Qualitative data. Lemmings is a puzzle-strategy game without traditional platformer physics. Movement parameters are skill-based rather than player-controlled. No detailed physics documentation available in public sources. Lemmings walk in predetermined patterns, fall under gravity, and interact with terrain through assigned skills rather than direct player input.
